title: selaginella brisbanensis
intro: Selaginella brisbanensis, also known as Australian spikemoss, is a type of small, non-vascular plant that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Selaginella brisbanensis can be made into a tea, infusion, or poultice to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
